questions.question: Explain the difference between dependent and independent variables in regression analysis.
keyPoints: Dependent variable is the target/output variable to be predicted
Independent variables are features/predictors used for prediction
Regression models the relationship: dependent = f(independent variables) + error

questions.question: What is overfitting in regression? How can it be detected and prevented?
keyPoints: Overfitting: model learns noise in training data
Detection: poor performance on test data despite good training performance
Prevention: cross-validation, regularization, reducing model complexity, more training data

questions.question: Define bias and variance. Explain the bias-variance tradeoff.
keyPoints: Bias: error from overly simplistic model assumptions
Variance: error from model being too sensitive to training data
Tradeoff: minimize total error by balancing bias and variance
High bias leads to underfitting, high variance leads to overfitting

questions.question: What is the purpose of feature scaling in regression? Name one technique used.
keyPoints: Feature scaling normalizes input features to similar ranges
Purpose: helps gradient descent converge faster and more reliably
Common techniques: Min-Max scaling or Z-score (standardization)

questions.question: Explain the concept of generalization in machine learning regression.
keyPoints: Generalization: model's ability to perform well on unseen data
Goal of regression: learn patterns that apply beyond training data
Evaluated using test data performance metrics
Poor generalization indicates overfitting

questions.question: What is a residual in regression analysis? How are residuals used to evaluate model quality?
keyPoints: Residual = Actual value - Predicted value
Residuals measure error for each prediction
Pattern in residual plot indicates model problems
Random scatter around zero indicates good fit

questions.question: Differentiate between regression and classification in machine learning.
keyPoints: Regression: predicts continuous numerical values
Classification: predicts discrete categorical labels
Regression output is a number; classification output is a class
Both are supervised learning techniques

questions.question: Explain the steps involved in building a regression model. Discuss how you would evaluate its performance.
keyPoints: Data collection and preprocessing
Feature selection and engineering
Model training using training data
Hyperparameter tuning
Model evaluation using metrics: MSE, RMSE, R-squared
Testing on held-out test data
Checking for overfitting/underfitting
Model deployment and monitoring

questions.question: Explain the concept of cost function in regression. How does gradient descent help in minimizing the cost function?
keyPoints: Cost function measures prediction error (e.g., Mean Squared Error)
Goal: find parameters that minimize cost function
Gradient descent: iterative optimization algorithm
Computes gradient (slope) and moves opposite direction
Learning rate controls step size
Converges to minimum (global or local)

questions.question: Discuss the problems of underfitting and overfitting in regression. How can these problems be addressed?
keyPoints: Underfitting: model too simple, fails to capture patterns
Overfitting: model too complex, learns noise
Signs: training vs test error comparison
Solutions for underfitting: add features, increase model complexity
Solutions for overfitting: regularization, reduce features, more data
Cross-validation for reliable evaluation

questions.question: What are outliers in regression? How do they affect regression models? Discuss methods to handle outliers.
keyPoints: Outliers: data points significantly different from others
Impact: disproportionately influence regression parameters
Detection: residual analysis, box plots, z-scores
Handling: remove outliers if errors, cap values, use robust regression
Decision depends on whether outliers are genuine or measurement errors
